items.title: F1 scrambles to change plans amid air travel chaos but ‘no impact’ to Melbourne race

items.content: Australian Grand Prix boss Travis Auld is confident all 22 Formula 1 drivers and their teams will be in Melbourne by Wednesday for the first race of the season, despite at least one team being forced to change travel plans.

items.title: CEO says F1 weekend will run without issue despite Middle East conflict

items.content: Formula 1's season opener is expected to go ahead without a hitch this weekend in Melbourne, after conflict in the Middle East forced a change in travel plans for many teams.

clusters.title: Penny Wong gives emotional speech as Pauline Hanson is censured in Australian Senate – video
clusters.neutral_headline: Hanson censured over no 'good' Muslims comment, Coalition votes against
bullet_summary: Senator Pauline Hanson has faced a censure motion over her comments to Sky News in February, where the One Nation leader said: 'You say, “Well, there’s good Muslims out there
Reported by Guardian Australia, ABC News Australia

items.title: Penny Wong gives emotional speech as Pauline Hanson is censured in Australian Senate – video
items.standfirst: Senator Pauline Hanson has faced a censure motion over her comments to Sky News in February, where the One Nation leader said: 'You say, “Well, there’s good Muslims out there.” How can you tell me there are good Muslims?' The leader of the government in the Senate, Penny Wong, said that ‘to claim that “none of them are good” is to say there are no good Australians amongst them; is to tell a child that no matter how hard they study, how kindly they treat others, how much they love this country, they will never belong.’ The Senate has voted 36 to 17 to censure Pauline Hanson over her comments about Muslim Australians.Australian politics updatesPauline Hanson expected to face censure motion in Senate over ‘inflammatory’ comments about Muslims Continue reading...
items.content: Senator Pauline Hanson has faced a censure motion over her comments to Sky News in February, where the One Nation leader said: 'You say, “Well, there’s good Muslims out there.” How can you tell me there are good Muslims?' The leader of the government in the Senate, Penny Wong, said that ‘to claim that “none of them are good” is to say there are no good Australians amongst them; is to tell a child that no matter how hard they study, how kindly they treat others, how much they love this country, they will never belong.’ The Senate has voted 36 to 17 to censure Pauline Hanson over her comments about Muslim Australians.Australian politics updatesPauline Hanson expected to face censure motion in Senate over ‘inflammatory’ comments about Muslims Continue reading...

items.title: Hanson censured over no 'good' Muslims comment, Coalition votes against
items.standfirst: Two Liberal senators cross the floor to vote for Labor's motion that censured the One Nation leader for "inflammatory and divisive comments" that sought to vilify Muslim Australians.
items.content: Two Liberal senators cross the floor to vote for Labor's motion that censured the One Nation leader for "inflammatory and divisive comments" that sought to vilify Muslim Australians.

items.content: As the world reacts to the death of Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, the Iranian women's football team prepares to play its opening Women's Asian Cup game in Australia.

items.title: State TV presenter cries while announcing the death of Iran's supreme leader
items.standfirst: An Iranian state media presenter broke down in tears as he announced the death of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
items.content: An Iranian state media presenter broke down in tears as he announced the death of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.The presenter told viewers the supreme leader, who was a key target in the US-Israel attacks, had "tasted the drink of martyrdom and joined the sublime heavenly kingdom of God" in a translated version of the broadcast.Others could be heard crying off-screen as the newsreader announced the news to the nation. LIVE UPDATES: New air attack warning sounds as fresh explosions heard READ MORE: Dubai Airport damaged in reported strike, four hurt Following the attack, President Donald Trump announced the leader had been killed.Iranian state media also confirmed that Khamenei is dead with a brief statement which said the supreme leader "has reached martyrdom".Khamenei, who has been supreme leader since 1989, was reportedly in his compound in Tehran when the joint assault was launched on military and government sites in Iran.Satellite images showed black smoke billowing from the compound, which appeared completely destroyed.NEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.Download the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter here
